creator: kit-ty-kate@outlook.com
text:
When the part of the upgrade to a newer release of FreeBSD fails (see for example https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=275847 ), there seems to be currently no way of installing the missing files other than by copying them manually from freebsd-src or similar sources.

To fix that I propose a new subcommand to freebsd-update: reinstall.

Calling `freebsd-update reinstall` would force reinstallation of the current release, making sure every files and directories are correctly installed in the process.

Such subcommand could also be called `fix` or something of the sort if you prefer.
